nodes.label: Qeynos Audio Suite Work Order
nodes.rationale: One VST at a time, each fully finished (DSP + GUI + presets + built VST3 + validated) before the next begins, so the run can stop at any point with everything completed so far usable in FL Studio.

nodes.label: Tech stack decisions (nih-plug / rustup-gnu / egui / pluginval)
nodes.rationale: nih-plug: one-command cargo builds, no Steinberg SDK setup, exports VST3 (FL's format), excellent for autonomous iteration. gnu toolchain: zero admin rights, self-contained linker (MSVC Build Tools fallback). nih_plug_egui: most reliable to build unattended, shared dark theme for one product family. pluginval: catches crashes/param bugs without FL open. Documents\VST3 install target avoids UAC prompts. FL runs all instances of one plugin DLL in one process, which is what makes the master/client mastering architecture possible via shared state.

nodes.label: GRIT (work order ask)
nodes.rationale: Goes first because it proves aux-input plumbing for later plugins.

nodes.label: EMBER (work order ask)
nodes.rationale: Hardest DSP of Phase 1 — scheduled second so the pipeline is proven first.

nodes.label: OVERSEER (work order ask)
nodes.rationale: Scheduled last in Phase 1 because it's an architecture, not a plugin — but before the catalog clones since it's a named want. Hosting Ozone inside would require a full VST3 host in-plugin: flagged stretch goal, not critical path.

nodes.label: Phase 2 — Lese catalog clones
nodes.rationale: Ordered simplest → hardest (DRIFT first, CHAMBER last). Frahm skipped: gestural processor is mostly a MIDI-controller UX play, low value cloned.

nodes.label: Definition of done (every plugin)
nodes.rationale: Clean release build bundled to .vst3, pluginval strictness 8 pass, installed to Documents\VST3 with README param reference, 5+ factory presets, suite-consistent egui GUI, one git commit per plugin — the repo is the changelog.

nodes.label: FL Studio MCP server (work order)
nodes.rationale: W4/W5 are bounded by MCP limitations (can't load plugins or create patterns), but naming/coloring/routing/tempo are exactly what it CAN do; W1-W3 use piano roll scripts instead because those run inside FL and persist notes properly.

edges.source_location: Phase 3 #5 'the analog counterpart to WIRE's digital degradation'
Phase 4 — same named entity described in both documents
